data.detail: awaiting-directive atom 0: awaiting Oscar's directive for atom 0

data.task:
Build Option B — a scoped, read-only `readGoverned(path)` tool for Oz, so the founder can ask Oz about governed flat files in-session (e.g. 'what does ADR-0017 say about the refresh verb?', 'show me the Oscar base persona') without launching an adhoc. Founder has RATIFIED Option B and explicitly REJECTED any table-of-contents / index / cached manifest: the repo is the single source of truth, so every lookup must read LIVE from disk — do NOT add any digest enrichment, generated index, or cache. DISCOVER FIRST (do not trust this description as exhaustive): - Read `packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts`: the `ToolName` union (~line 64), `validateToolCall()` (~lines 297-319), `toolInstructions()` (~line 226), and `executeTool()` dispatch (~line 378). Match the exact pattern the existing read-only verbs (`show`, `status`, `refresh`) use. - Read `packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts` `executeOzCommand()` to see how a command kind is dispatched to an op + reply renderer. - Read how an existing op is implemented in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` (e.g. the `show`/`status` handlers) for the file-read + reply shape. - Read `packages/core/src/write-scope/glob.ts` + `partition.ts`: `matchesAny(path, globs)` is exported from `@cocoder/core` and tested — REUSE it for the scope guard; write no new glob code. WHAT TO BUILD: 1. Define a `GOVERNED_READ_SCOPE` allowlist constant (single source of truth for this surface) covering the governed zones named in the Objective: `cocoder/decisions/**`, `cocoder/priorities/**`, `cocoder/personas/**`, `packages/personas/base/**` — plus any of `cocoder/standards/**`, `cocoder/playbooks/**` that actually exist in the tree (verify with ls; include only real directories). Put it where other write-scope constants live (e.g. alongside `OZ_ACTION_SCOPE` in `packages/core/src/write-scope/`). 2. Add a `read-governed` tool to Oz's surface end-to-end: add to `ToolName`, add a `validateToolCall()` case that requires a string `path`, add a `toolInstructions()` entry describing the JSON signature so Oz knows how to call it, and wire `executeTool()` + the `oz-chat.ts` dispatch to the handler. 3. Implement the handler (async, in launcher.ts following the existing op pattern): resolve/normalize the requested path RELATIVE TO THE REPO ROOT, reject path traversal (`..`) and absolute escapes, then guard with `matchesAny(normalizedPath, GOVERNED_READ_SCOPE)`. If the path is outside the governed zones, return a clear error WITHOUT reading the file (e.g. `Path "

" is outside the governed zones Oz may read.`). If allowed, read the file from disk and return its content. This is READ-ONLY: no write, no commit, no mutation. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: - Oz has a `read-governed` tool that, given a governed path, returns the live file content read from disk (no index/cache). - A path OUTSIDE the governed zones is REJECTED with a clear error and the file is NOT read. This must explicitly cover: product code (`packages/core/src/...`, `packages/daemon/src/...`), workspace/run state (run records, event streams), and path-traversal attempts (`cocoder/decisions/../../packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts`). - Tests pin BOTH directions: an in-zone read returns the expected content for at least one real governed file (e.g. an ADR under cocoder/decisions/), and out-of-zone + traversal paths are rejected. Reuse the existing test style under `packages/daemon/tests/` and/or `packages/core/tests/` (see `oz-action-scope.test.ts` for the scope-check pattern). - `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec tsc --noEmit` and the daemon typecheck are clean; the affected daemon + core test suites pass. WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / BOUNDARY: - Read-only ONLY. Do NOT grant Oz any write/commit path to governed files through this surface — repair writes remain the existing repair verb. - Do NOT add a TOC, index, digest enrichment, or cache (founder-rejected; repo is SSOT — read live every time). - Do NOT touch product code beyond what wiring the tool requires, and do NOT change existing verb behavior or `deriveWrapDisposition`. - The scope guard must be a real allowlist (matchesAny), not a denylist; default-deny anything not under a governed zone. Write-scope: `packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts`, `packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts`, `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`, the new/edited scope constant under `packages/core/src/write-scope/`, and colocated tests under `packages/daemon/tests/` and/or `packages/core/tests/`. Report the exact files changed, the scope-guard logic, the test command(s) you ran, and their output.
